title: aulomyrcia polymorpha
intro: Aulomyrcia polymorpha, also known as the Pará pequi, is a plant species belonging to the family Myrtaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The medicinal properties of Aulomyrcia polymorpha include its rich content of essential oils, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits associated with this plant include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wound healing and skin protection. Aulomyrcia polymorpha is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which are traditionally used in folk medicine in its native regions.
